text: This minimally invasive technique has already been performed on children with birth defects
at a handful of hospitals around the country.

The way it works, instead of making an incision several inches long in the chest, doctors use a five millimeter camera and instruments as small as three millimeters in diameter to make three tiny incisions and essentially do the same procedure.
But since the technique has only been around for the past several years, there is a learning curve.
But with time, I think both operations, whether it's the lung or the esophagus, are just the same as they are open.
